indicators.name: Proportion of population below the international poverty line
indicators.definition: Proportion of the population living below the international poverty line of $2.15 per day (2017 PPP), disaggregated by sex, age, employment status, and geographical location
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of people living below $2.15/day / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: National household income and expenditure surveys, World Bank PovcalNet
indicators.data_collection_method: Household survey with consumption/income module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, urban/rural, employment status

indicators.name: Prevalence of undernourishment
indicators.definition: Proportion of the population whose habitual food consumption is insufficient to provide the dietary energy levels required to maintain a normal active and healthy life
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of undernourished people / Total population) × 100, based on dietary energy supply adequacy
indicators.data_source: FAO Food Balance Sheets, national household food consumption surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: Food balance sheets combined with food consumption survey data
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, geographic region

indicators.name: Maternal mortality ratio
indicators.definition: Number of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births during a given time period, from any cause related to or aggravated by pregnancy or its management
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of maternal deaths / Number of live births) × 100,000
indicators.data_source: Civil registration and vital statistics systems, health facility records, household surveys (DHS/MICS)
indicators.data_collection_method: Vital registration, reproductive age mortality studies, verbal autopsy
indicators.disaggregation_fields: age group, cause of death, geographic region, facility vs community delivery

indicators.name: Proportion of children and young people achieving minimum proficiency in reading and mathematics
indicators.definition: Percentage of children and young people at the end of primary and lower secondary education achieving at least minimum proficiency level in reading and mathematics
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of children achieving minimum proficiency / Total number of children assessed) × 100
indicators.data_source: National and cross-national learning assessments (PIRLS, TIMSS, PISA, PASEC, SACMEQ)
indicators.data_collection_method: Standardised learning assessment administered in schools
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, urban/rural, wealth quintile, disability status, grade level

indicators.name: Proportion of seats held by women in national parliaments and local governments
indicators.definition: Proportion of seats held by women in national parliaments (lower and upper chambers) and local government bodies
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of seats held by women / Total number of seats) × 100
indicators.data_source: Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU), national electoral commissions
indicators.data_collection_method: Administrative records from parliaments and electoral bodies
indicators.disaggregation_fields: level of government (national/local), chamber (upper/lower)

indicators.name: Proportion of population using safely managed drinking water services
indicators.definition: Proportion of the population using an improved drinking water source that is located on premises, available when needed, and free from faecal and priority chemical contamination
indicators.calculation_method: (Population using safely managed drinking water / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme (JMP), national household surveys (DHS/MICS)
indicators.data_collection_method: Household surveys with water quality testing module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, wealth quintile, geographic region

indicators.name: Proportion of population with access to electricity
indicators.definition: Percentage of the population with access to electricity, measured by household connections to the national grid, mini-grids, or stand-alone solar systems
indicators.calculation_method: (Population with electricity access / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank Global Electrification Database, national utility records, household surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: Household survey and utility administrative records
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, type of access (grid/off-grid), reliability tier

indicators.name: Proportion of informal employment in total employment
indicators.definition: Proportion of total employment that is classified as informal employment, encompassing workers in the informal sector and informal employment outside the informal sector
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of persons in informal employment / Total employment) × 100
indicators.data_source: National labour force surveys, ILO harmonised estimates
indicators.data_collection_method: Labour force survey with informality module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, sector of economic activity, urban/rural

indicators.name: Proportion of population living in households with access to basic services
indicators.definition: Proportion of population living in households with access to basic services including improved water, sanitation, electricity, clean cooking fuels, and adequate housing
indicators.calculation_method: (Population in households with access to all basic services / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: National household surveys (DHS/MICS), census data
indicators.data_collection_method: Multi-topic household survey
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, income quintile, type of service

indicators.name: Under-five mortality rate
indicators.definition: Probability of dying between birth and the fifth birthday, expressed per 1,000 live births
indicators.calculation_method: Estimated using direct or indirect methods from survey or vital registration data; probability of dying before age 5 per 1,000 live births
indicators.data_source: UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (UN IGME), DHS, MICS, vital registration
indicators.data_collection_method: Birth history data from household surveys or vital registration systems
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, urban/rural, wealth quintile, mother's education

indicators.name: Proportion of population using safely managed sanitation services
indicators.definition: Proportion of the population using an improved sanitation facility that is not shared with other households and where excreta are safely disposed of in situ or transported and treated off-site
indicators.calculation_method: (Population using safely managed sanitation / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: WHO/UNICEF JMP, national household surveys, census data
indicators.data_collection_method: Household surveys with sanitation observation module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, wealth quintile, type of sanitation facility

indicators.name: Number of deaths, missing persons and directly affected persons attributed to disasters per 100,000 population
indicators.definition: Number of people who died, went missing, or were directly affected by disasters per 100,000 population in a given year
indicators.calculation_method: (Deaths + missing + directly affected persons from disasters / Total population) × 100,000
indicators.data_source: EM-DAT International Disaster Database, national disaster management agencies
indicators.data_collection_method: Administrative records from national disaster management authorities
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of disaster, sex, age group, geographic region

indicators.name: Proportion of population who believe decision-making is inclusive and responsive
indicators.definition: Proportion of population who believe decision-making at national and local levels is inclusive and responsive to their needs
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of respondents reporting inclusive decision-making / Total respondents) × 100
indicators.data_source: National governance perception surveys, Afrobarometer, Latinobarómetro
indicators.data_collection_method: Population-based perception survey
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, urban/rural, income level, level of government

indicators.name: Proportion of population covered by social protection floors/systems
indicators.definition: Proportion of the population covered by at least one social protection benefit (cash transfers, pensions, unemployment benefits, disability benefits, maternal/child benefits)
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of persons receiving at least one social protection benefit / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: ILO World Social Protection Database, national social protection registries
indicators.data_collection_method: Administrative records from social protection agencies and household surveys
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, type of benefit, urban/rural

indicators.name: Net enrolment rate in primary education
indicators.definition: Total number of students of official primary school age enrolled in primary education, expressed as a percentage of the corresponding population
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of children of official primary age enrolled in primary / Population of official primary school age) × 100
indicators.data_source: UNESCO Institute for Statistics, national education management information systems (EMIS)
indicators.data_collection_method: School census and administrative records from EMIS
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, urban/rural, wealth quintile, disability status

indicators.name: Crop yield change among smallholder farmers
indicators.definition: Percentage change in yield of targeted crops among smallholder farmers supported by the project, measured in metric tonnes per hectare
indicators.calculation_method: ((Yield at endline - Yield at baseline) / Yield at baseline) × 100, measured in MT/ha for primary crop
indicators.data_source: Project baseline and endline surveys, crop cutting exercises, farmer records
indicators.data_collection_method: Crop cutting surveys with randomly selected plots; farmer self-reporting validated by field measurements
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex of household head, crop type, irrigated/rainfed, farm size category

indicators.name: Persons with new access to financial services
indicators.definition: Number of persons in project areas gaining access to financial services (savings, credit, insurance, remittances) for the first time as a result of the intervention
indicators.calculation_method: Count of individuals who report having gained access to at least one financial service that they did not have before the project
indicators.data_source: Project MIS, financial institution records, beneficiary surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: Beneficiary registration and follow-up survey; cross-referenced with financial institution records
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, type of financial service, indigenous/non-indigenous

indicators.name: Households reporting improved access to markets
indicators.definition: Number and percentage of households reporting improved physical and economic access to markets (reduced travel time, reduced post-harvest losses, improved market information) as a result of the intervention
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of HH reporting improved market access / Total targeted HH) × 100; absolute count also reported
indicators.data_source: Beneficiary outcome surveys, project MIS, market access assessments
indicators.data_collection_method: Structured household survey with market access module at baseline and endline
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex of household head, distance to market, type of market improvement

indicators.name: Persons trained in income-generating activities or business management
indicators.definition: Number of persons who have completed training in income-generating activities, business management, or entrepreneurship supported by the project
indicators.calculation_method: Count of individuals who have completed at least 80% of a training course in IGA or business management
indicators.data_source: Training attendance records, project MIS, certification records
indicators.data_collection_method: Training registration and completion tracking through project MIS
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, type of training, youth/non-youth

indicators.name: Land under improved management practices
indicators.definition: Hectares of farmland brought under improved agricultural practices (including climate-smart techniques, sustainable intensification, soil conservation) as a result of the project
indicators.calculation_method: Sum of hectares where verified adoption of improved practices has occurred, measured through field observation and farmer reporting
indicators.data_source: Project field monitoring, GPS-mapped farm plots, extension worker records
indicators.data_collection_method: Field observation with GPS mapping; extension officer verification visits
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of practice adopted, crop type, irrigated/rainfed, farm size

indicators.name: Rural roads constructed or rehabilitated
indicators.definition: Kilometres of rural roads constructed, rehabilitated, or improved to an all-weather standard as a result of the project
indicators.calculation_method: Sum of km of road constructed or rehabilitated to all-weather standard, verified by engineering inspection
indicators.data_source: Project engineering records, contractor reports, independent verification
indicators.data_collection_method: Engineering progress monitoring with independent quality verification
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of work (new/rehabilitation), road classification, surface type

indicators.name: Households adopting environmentally sustainable and climate-resilient technologies
indicators.definition: Number of households that have adopted environmentally sustainable and climate-resilient technologies or practices promoted by the project
indicators.calculation_method: Count of households verified to have adopted at least one promoted climate-resilient technology or practice
indicators.data_source: Household surveys, project field monitoring, extension service records
indicators.data_collection_method: Household survey with technology adoption checklist; field verification by extension agents
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex of household head, type of technology, agro-ecological zone

indicators.name: Persons in supported rural enterprises reporting increased profit
indicators.definition: Number of persons in rural enterprises supported by the project who report increased net profit compared to baseline
indicators.calculation_method: Count of enterprise operators reporting net profit increase ≥10% compared to baseline, validated through financial records or structured interview
indicators.data_source: Enterprise surveys, project beneficiary tracking, financial records
indicators.data_collection_method: Structured enterprise survey with simplified profit/loss accounting
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, enterprise type, youth-led

indicators.name: Water users with improved irrigation services
indicators.definition: Number of water users who have been provided with new or improved irrigation and water management services through project-supported infrastructure
indicators.calculation_method: Count of registered water users in project-supported irrigation schemes that meet service delivery standards
indicators.data_source: Water user association records, project MIS, scheme management data
indicators.data_collection_method: Registration and service delivery monitoring through water user associations
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, type of irrigation system, scheme size

indicators.name: Households with improved food security (reduced FIES score)
indicators.definition: Number and percentage of project-supported households that have improved their food security status as measured by a reduction in the Food Insecurity Experience Scale (FIES) score
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of HH with improved FIES / Total targeted HH) × 100; uses 8-item FIES module comparing baseline to endline
indicators.data_source: Household surveys using FIES module, project baseline/endline studies
indicators.data_collection_method: Household survey with validated FIES 8-question module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex of household head, severity level (moderate/severe), urban/rural

indicators.name: Grassland and cropland groups with secure tenure rights
indicators.definition: Number of community groups or individuals who have received formalised or strengthened tenure rights over grassland and cropland as a result of the project
indicators.calculation_method: Count of groups/individuals with documented tenure security (certificate, title, or formal recognition) issued during the project period
indicators.data_source: Land administration records, project MIS, community records
indicators.data_collection_method: Administrative records review and community-level verification
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of tenure instrument, sex of holder, land use type

indicators.name: Prevalence of moderate or severe food insecurity (FIES)
indicators.definition: Percentage of the population experiencing moderate or severe food insecurity based on the Food Insecurity Experience Scale, reflecting uncertain access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food
indicators.calculation_method: Prevalence estimated using Rasch model applied to FIES 8-item questionnaire responses; population weighted
indicators.data_source: FAO Voices of the Hungry project, Gallup World Poll, national household surveys with FIES module
indicators.data_collection_method: Population-representative household survey with FIES 8-item module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, urban/rural, income level

indicators.name: Average value of food production per labour unit
indicators.definition: Value of agricultural production (in constant USD) per person engaged in agriculture, measuring labour productivity in the food production sector
indicators.calculation_method: Total gross value of agricultural output (constant prices) / Total number of persons engaged in agriculture
indicators.data_source: FAO Statistical Database (FAOSTAT), national agricultural census data
indicators.data_collection_method: Agricultural census and production surveys combined with labour statistics
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, type of production (crop/livestock/fisheries), farm size

indicators.name: Proportion of agricultural area under productive and sustainable agriculture
indicators.definition: Proportion of total agricultural area that is managed using sustainable practices, including soil health management, water use efficiency, biodiversity conservation, and reduced chemical inputs
indicators.calculation_method: (Agricultural area meeting sustainability criteria / Total agricultural area) × 100
indicators.data_source: FAO Land Use Statistics, national agricultural surveys, remote sensing data
indicators.data_collection_method: Farm survey with sustainability practice checklist; validated with remote sensing analysis
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of sustainability practice, farm size, agro-ecological zone

indicators.name: Global food loss index
indicators.definition: Food losses from production to retail level (excluding retail and consumer level) expressed as a percentage of total food produced, aggregated across commodity groups
indicators.calculation_method: Weighted average of commodity-specific loss percentages, using economic value of production as weights
indicators.data_source: FAO Food Loss and Waste Database, national post-harvest loss assessments
indicators.data_collection_method: Supply chain loss measurement studies at critical loss points; national loss assessment surveys
indicators.disaggregation_fields: commodity group, stage of supply chain, region

indicators.name: Incidence of land tenure insecurity
indicators.definition: Proportion of the adult population with insecure tenure rights to land, measured by legally recognised documentation and perceived tenure security
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of adults reporting insecure tenure / Total adult population surveyed) × 100; combines documentation status and perception measures
indicators.data_source: FAO land tenure survey data, national household surveys, World Bank LSMS
indicators.data_collection_method: Household survey with land tenure module covering documentation and perception questions
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, type of tenure, urban/rural, indigenous status

indicators.name: Number of plant and animal genetic resources secured in conservation facilities
indicators.definition: Total number of plant genetic resources for food and agriculture (PGRFA) and animal genetic resources secured in medium- or long-term conservation facilities (gene banks)
indicators.calculation_method: Count of unique accessions held in gene banks meeting FAO standards for medium- or long-term conservation
indicators.data_source: FAO World Information and Early Warning System (WIEWS), national gene bank records
indicators.data_collection_method: Gene bank inventory and reporting to WIEWS
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type (plant/animal), crop/species group, conservation type (ex-situ/in-situ)

indicators.name: Dietary energy supply adequacy
indicators.definition: Average dietary energy supply expressed as a percentage of the average dietary energy requirement in the country, reflecting national food availability
indicators.calculation_method: (Average dietary energy supply per capita / Average dietary energy requirement per capita) × 100
indicators.data_source: FAO Food Balance Sheets, national food composition tables
indicators.data_collection_method: Compilation of food balance sheets using production, trade, and utilisation data
indicators.disaggregation_fields: food group, geographic region

indicators.name: Proportion of small-scale food producers with access to productive resources
indicators.definition: Proportion of small-scale food producers who have access to land, livestock, machinery, irrigation, extension, technology, and financial services
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of small-scale food producers with access to set of productive resources / Total small-scale food producers) × 100
indicators.data_source: FAO integrated agricultural surveys, national household surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: Agricultural household survey with access-to-resources module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, type of resource, farm size, agro-ecological zone

indicators.name: Anomalies in food prices
indicators.definition: Indicator of food price anomalies identifying abnormally high food prices relative to historical patterns, used as an early warning for food crises
indicators.calculation_method: Compound Growth Rate applied to price time series; anomaly identified when current price exceeds upper bound of historical distribution (mean + 1 standard deviation of quarterly growth rates)
indicators.data_source: FAO FPMA Global Food Price Monitoring, national market information systems
indicators.data_collection_method: Systematic price monitoring from wholesale and retail markets
indicators.disaggregation_fields: commodity, market location, geographic region

indicators.name: Number of people receiving food assistance
indicators.definition: Total number of food-insecure people who received food assistance (in-kind, cash, or voucher-based) through the programme during the reporting period
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of unique beneficiaries receiving at least one food assistance distribution during the reporting period
indicators.data_source: Programme beneficiary registration databases, distribution monitoring records
indicators.data_collection_method: Beneficiary registration with biometric or ID-based deduplication at distribution points
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, transfer modality (food/cash/voucher), displacement status

indicators.name: Number of children under five reached by nutrition-specific interventions
indicators.definition: Number of children under five years of age reached by USG-supported nutrition-specific interventions including growth monitoring, vitamin A supplementation, treatment of acute malnutrition, and complementary feeding programmes
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of children under 5 who received at least one nutrition-specific intervention during the reporting period
indicators.data_source: Health facility records, community health worker registers, programme MIS
indicators.data_collection_method: Health facility reporting and community-based nutrition programme tracking
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age (0-6m, 6-23m, 24-59m), type of intervention

indicators.name: Number of farmers who have applied improved technologies or management practices
indicators.definition: Number of farmers who have applied improved technologies or management practices with USG assistance, covering crop genetics, cultural practices, pest management, post-harvest handling, soil management, irrigation, and livestock management
indicators.calculation_method: Count of farmers verified to have applied at least one promoted technology or practice on their farm during the current agricultural season
indicators.data_source: Farmer surveys, extension agent monitoring reports, programme beneficiary tracking
indicators.data_collection_method: Farmer-level survey with technology adoption checklist; verified by extension agent observation
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, technology type, value chain, farm size category

indicators.name: Value of annual sales of producers and firms receiving USG assistance
indicators.definition: Total value of annual sales of farms and firms that received USG assistance, measuring economic growth and market engagement among programme beneficiaries
indicators.calculation_method: Sum of gross annual sales reported by all producers and firms receiving USG assistance, converted to USD at market exchange rates
indicators.data_source: Beneficiary enterprise surveys, programme MIS, market transaction records
indicators.data_collection_method: Enterprise-level survey with sales recordkeeping module; validated with market records where available
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex of enterprise owner, value chain, enterprise size, new/continuing

indicators.name: Number of people with improved access to drinking water supply
indicators.definition: Number of people who gained access to an improved drinking water source or whose existing water service level was improved as a result of USG assistance
indicators.calculation_method: Population served by new or improved water supply infrastructure, calculated using standard per-capita design parameters for each system type
indicators.data_source: Infrastructure completion records, community water point mapping, household survey validation
indicators.data_collection_method: Engineering completion reports combined with population estimates; validated by household spot checks
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, type of water system, new/improved access

indicators.name: Number of laws, policies, or procedures drafted, proposed, or adopted to promote gender equality
indicators.definition: Number of laws, policies, regulations, or procedures drafted, proposed, or adopted with USG assistance that are designed to promote gender equality or prevent/respond to gender-based violence
indicators.calculation_method: Count of distinct legal/policy instruments at any stage (drafted, proposed, adopted) that address gender equality, with USG technical or financial assistance
indicators.data_source: Legislative tracking databases, government gazette records, programme reports
indicators.data_collection_method: Systematic tracking of legislative and policy processes with documentary evidence
indicators.disaggregation_fields: stage (drafted/proposed/adopted), level (national/sub-national), thematic area

indicators.name: Number of persons trained in child health and nutrition through USG-supported programmes
indicators.definition: Number of health workers, caregivers, and community members trained in child health and nutrition through USG-supported health programmes
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of individuals completing at least one USG-supported training in child health or nutrition during the reporting period
indicators.data_source: Training records, programme MIS, post-training assessment results
indicators.data_collection_method: Training attendance tracking with pre/post assessment; linked to programme MIS
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, cadre (health worker/community volunteer/caregiver), type of training

indicators.name: Number of judicial personnel trained with USG assistance
indicators.definition: Number of judges, prosecutors, public defenders, court administrators, and other judicial personnel who completed USG-supported training on rule of law, human rights, or justice sector reform
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of judicial sector personnel completing USG-supported training during the reporting period
indicators.data_source: Training records, programme MIS, judicial academy records
indicators.data_collection_method: Training registration, attendance tracking, and completion certification
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, type of personnel, topic of training, level of court

indicators.name: Number of hectares under improved natural resource management
indicators.definition: Number of hectares of biologically significant areas under improved natural resource management as a result of USG assistance, including forests, wetlands, and marine/coastal areas
indicators.calculation_method: Sum of hectares with documented implementation of improved NRM practices, verified by remote sensing and/or field inspection
indicators.data_source: Project GIS databases, remote sensing analysis, protected area management records
indicators.data_collection_method: GIS-based spatial monitoring combined with field verification of management practices
indicators.disaggregation_fields: ecosystem type, management practice, tenure type, protection status

indicators.name: Number of learners enrolled in primary and secondary education with USG support
indicators.definition: Total number of learners enrolled in USG-supported primary and secondary education programmes or schools during the reporting period
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of learners enrolled in USG-supported schools or programmes at the start of the academic year
indicators.data_source: School enrolment records, EMIS data, programme MIS
indicators.data_collection_method: School-level enrolment data collection linked to national EMIS
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, education level (primary/secondary), grade, disability status, displacement status

indicators.name: Number of micro, small, and medium enterprises supported
indicators.definition: Number of micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) that received business development services, access to finance, or other USG enterprise support
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of enterprises receiving at least one type of USG business support during the reporting period
indicators.data_source: Programme beneficiary databases, financial institution records, service provider reports
indicators.data_collection_method: Enterprise registration and service delivery tracking through programme MIS
indicators.disaggregation_fields: enterprise size (micro/small/medium), sector, sex of owner, urban/rural

indicators.name: Number of social assistance beneficiaries participating in productive safety nets with USG support
indicators.definition: Number of individuals participating in USG-supported productive safety net programmes that combine social assistance transfers with livelihood support, public works, or human capital investments
indicators.calculation_method: Deduplicated count of individuals enrolled in productive safety net programmes with both transfer and productive components
indicators.data_source: Social protection programme MIS, beneficiary registries, payroll records
indicators.data_collection_method: Programme registry with biometric or ID-linked tracking of participation in both transfer and productive components
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, type of productive component, disability status

indicators.name: Poverty headcount ratio at national poverty lines
indicators.definition: Percentage of the population living below the national poverty line, as defined by each country's own poverty measurement methodology
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of people below the national poverty line / Total population) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank World Development Indicators, national household income/expenditure surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: National household survey with income or consumption expenditure module
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, sex of household head, region, age group

indicators.name: Life expectancy at birth
indicators.definition: Average number of years a newborn would be expected to live if current mortality rates at the time of birth were to stay the same throughout the child's life
indicators.calculation_method: Derived from age-specific mortality rates using standard life table methods
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, UN Population Division, national vital registration systems
indicators.data_collection_method: Vital registration data and demographic survey estimates compiled by UN Population Division
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, urban/rural

indicators.name: Gross enrolment ratio in secondary education
indicators.definition: Total enrolment in secondary education, regardless of age, expressed as a percentage of the population of official secondary education age
indicators.calculation_method: (Total secondary enrolment / Population of official secondary school age) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, UNESCO Institute for Statistics, national EMIS
indicators.data_collection_method: School census administrative data combined with population estimates
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, lower/upper secondary

indicators.name: GDP per capita, PPP (current international $)
indicators.definition: Gross domestic product per capita converted to international dollars using purchasing power parity rates, representing the average economic output per person adjusted for price differences across countries
indicators.calculation_method: GDP (PPP, current international $) / Midyear population
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, International Comparison Program, IMF World Economic Outlook
indicators.data_collection_method: National accounts compilation using International Comparison Program PPP conversion factors
indicators.disaggregation_fields: not typically disaggregated (national-level aggregate)

indicators.name: Prevalence of stunting among children under five
indicators.definition: Percentage of children under five years of age whose height-for-age z-score is below minus two standard deviations from the WHO Child Growth Standards median
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of children under 5 with height-for-age z-score < -2 SD / Total children under 5 measured) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, UNICEF/WHO/World Bank Joint Malnutrition Estimates, DHS, MICS
indicators.data_collection_method: Anthropometric measurements (length/height) in nationally representative household surveys
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age (0-5m, 6-11m, 12-23m, 24-35m, 36-47m, 48-59m), urban/rural, wealth quintile

indicators.name: Access to electricity in rural areas
indicators.definition: Percentage of the rural population with access to electricity through national grid connections, mini-grids, or stand-alone systems
indicators.calculation_method: (Rural population with electricity access / Total rural population) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank Global Electrification Database, national utility data, household surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: Household surveys combined with utility administrative records and geospatial modelling
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of supply (grid/mini-grid/solar home system), service reliability tier

indicators.name: Immunisation, DPT (% of children ages 12-23 months)
indicators.definition: Percentage of children aged 12-23 months who received three doses of diphtheria, pertussis, and tetanus (DPT3) vaccine before their first birthday
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of children 12-23 months vaccinated with DPT3 / Total children 12-23 months) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, WHO/UNICEF Estimates of National Immunisation Coverage (WUENIC)
indicators.data_collection_method: Administrative coverage reports from health facilities and vaccination cards; validated with household survey data
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, urban/rural, wealth quintile, mother's education

indicators.name: Forest area as a proportion of total land area
indicators.definition: Proportion of total land area covered by forest, defined as land spanning more than 0.5 hectares with trees higher than 5 metres and a canopy cover of more than 10 percent
indicators.calculation_method: (Total forest area in hectares / Total land area in hectares) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, FAO Global Forest Resources Assessment (FRA)
indicators.data_collection_method: National forest inventory data submitted to FAO FRA; supplemented by remote sensing analysis
indicators.disaggregation_fields: forest type (natural/planted), primary/modified/production forest

indicators.name: Government effectiveness index
indicators.definition: Composite index capturing perceptions of the quality of public services, the quality of the civil service and its independence from political pressures, the quality of policy formulation and implementation, and the credibility of the government's commitment to such policies
indicators.calculation_method: Composite of over 30 individual data sources aggregated using an unobserved components model; standardised to range from approximately -2.5 to +2.5
indicators.data_source: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators (WGI), expert surveys, citizen perception surveys
indicators.data_collection_method: Aggregation of perception data from enterprise surveys, expert assessments, and citizen surveys from multiple sources
indicators.disaggregation_fields: not typically disaggregated (national-level composite)

indicators.name: Domestic credit to private sector (% of GDP)
indicators.definition: Financial resources provided to the private sector by financial corporations through loans, purchases of non-equity securities, trade credits, and other accounts receivable that establish a claim for repayment, expressed as a percentage of GDP
indicators.calculation_method: (Total domestic credit to private sector / GDP) × 100
indicators.data_source: World Bank WDI, IMF International Financial Statistics, central bank reports
indicators.data_collection_method: Monetary and financial statistics compiled from central bank and financial institution reporting
indicators.disaggregation_fields: type of financial institution, sector of borrower

indicators.name: Proportion of women aged 20-24 who were first married or in union before age 18
indicators.definition: Proportion of women aged 20-24 years who were first married or in a consensual union before age 18, measuring child marriage prevalence
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of women 20-24 first married/in union before age 18 / Total women aged 20-24) × 100
indicators.data_source: DHS, MICS, national household surveys, UNICEF global databases
indicators.data_collection_method: Household survey with marriage/union history module administered to women aged 20-24
indicators.disaggregation_fields: urban/rural, wealth quintile, education level, geographic region

indicators.name: Number of people in rural areas trained in financial literacy
indicators.definition: Number of rural men and women who completed financial literacy training supported by the project, covering basic money management, savings, credit, and insurance concepts
indicators.calculation_method: Count of individuals who completed at least 80% of a financial literacy training course, tracked through attendance and post-training assessment records
indicators.data_source: Training records, project MIS, financial service provider records
indicators.data_collection_method: Training registration and completion tracking with pre/post knowledge assessment
indicators.disaggregation_fields: sex, age group, youth/non-youth, literacy level

indicators.name: Proportion of countries with high levels of consumer food price inflation
indicators.definition: Proportion of countries where year-on-year consumer food price inflation exceeds overall consumer price inflation, indicating disproportionate food price increases affecting food access for vulnerable populations
indicators.calculation_method: (Number of countries where food CPI growth > overall CPI growth / Total countries monitored) × 100
indicators.data_source: FAO food price monitoring system, national statistics offices, ILO consumer price data
indicators.data_collection_method: Systematic collection and comparison of food CPI and general CPI from national statistical agencies
indicators.disaggregation_fields: income group, geographic region, level of food import dependence
